annotations.anchor_quote: 1. Societal barriers and poverty Case in point: (1) Prioritizing boy's education over girl's (2) Not having the means to support education.
annotations.correction: 1. Societal barriers and poverty: Analyze how prioritizing boys' education and lack of financial means perpetuate blind obedience through limited female empowerment. Case in point: ...
annotations.comment: Sub-points are merely descriptive lists lacking analytical depth; add cause-effect links to blind obedience for stronger argumentation.

annotations.anchor_quote: (2) In Pakistan 18% girls in age 12-16 get married.
annotations.correction: (2) In Pakistan, 18% of girls aged 12-16 get married (cite source, e.g., UNICEF).
annotations.comment: Statistic lacks source attribution; cite reliable source like UNICEF to verify and strengthen credibility.

annotations.anchor_quote: Appaling sanitation conditions ingovernment schools
annotations.correction: Appalling sanitation conditions in government schools
annotations.comment: Spelling and spacing errors ('Appaling', 'ingovernment') reduce clarity; correct to 'Appalling sanitation conditions in government schools'.

annotations.anchor_quote: No sofe transport for female studends from for flung areas.
annotations.correction: No safe transport for female students from far-flung areas.
annotations.comment: Multiple spelling errors ('sofe', 'studends', 'for flung') hinder readability; correct to 'No safe transport for female students from far-flung areas'.

annotations.anchor_quote: 5. Massive digital in present age of online learning. Case in Point: Global digital divide 68%, for women 51% (3) Pakistan female digital divide 24% whereas mode's is 48%
annotations.comment: Sub-points list descriptive statistics without analytical depth; explain how addressing digital divide ends blind obedience.

annotations.anchor_quote: 6. Formales lack of security outside the four walls of their home.
annotations.correction: 6. Females lack of security outside the four walls of their home.
annotations.comment: Misspelled 'Formale' as 'Females'; correct spelling to enhance readability.

annotations.anchor_quote: these impediment in way of women education.
annotations.correction: these impediments in the way of women education.
annotations.comment: Use plural 'impediments' and add article 'the'; correct grammar for clarity.

annotations.anchor_quote: Ensure "altidunal" change rather than more LawsD TAIMOOR BUKHARI
annotations.correction: Ensure "attitudinal" change rather than more laws.
annotations.comment: Misspelled 'altidunal' as 'attitudinal' and capitalize 'laws' properly; fix for professionalism.

annotations.anchor_quote: (1) 1/3 women globally have suffered sexual harassment (UN)
annotations.comment: Vague attribution '(UN)' for statistic; specify full source for verifiability.

annotations.anchor_quote: II. How strengthening the female mind by enlarging it with education Godsends blind obedience: 1. Only educated female breaks free from the shackles of patriarchal mind set that demands blind obedience. 2. Educated female secures economic independence which protects from obeying mindlessly 3. Females strengthened by education lencew their rights and do not submit to injustice.
annotations.correction: Develop sub-points with cause-effect analysis and evidence instead of description.
annotations.comment: Sub-points in outline are merely descriptive and lack analytical depth; add critical analysis through cause-effect relationships.

annotations.anchor_quote: I. Introduction It is an undeniable reality that by enlarging the female mind with education, there will be end to blind obedience, yet there are Various reasons which inhibit the process; consequently women across the globe suffer detrimental impacts. Hence, pragmatic policies and actions are needed to stream line women education
annotations.correction: Explicitly state thesis with stance on education ending obedience and roadmap of benefits, impediments, measures.
annotations.comment: Introduction in outline lacks explicit thesis and overview of main arguments; provide clear stance and structure preview.

annotations.anchor_quote: Godsends blind obedience
annotations.correction: ends
annotations.comment: 'Godsends' is incorrect word choice; replace with 'ends' to accurately state education terminates blind obedience.

annotations.anchor_quote: there will be end to blind obedience
annotations.correction: there will be an end to blind obedience
annotations.comment: Missing article; insert 'an' before 'end' for grammatical correctness.

annotations.anchor_quote: It has, undeniably, wisely phrased that it needs education to break free females from blind obedience.
annotations.correction: It is undeniably true that education is needed to free females from blind obedience.
annotations.comment: Introduction rephrases prompt awkwardly and lacks explicit thesis or roadmap of main arguments; state clear stance and outline structure (benefits, impediments, measures).

annotations.anchor_quote: Any one who is educated, questions, thinks, rationalizes and only then make an action.
annotations.correction: Any one who is educated questions, thinks, rationalizes, and only then makes an action.
annotations.comment: Subject-verb agreement error and punctuation issues; change 'make' to 'makes' and add commas in list.

annotations.anchor_quote: When female are educated, they do not act blindly or follow mindlessly.
annotations.correction: When females are educated, they do not act blindly or follow mindlessly.
annotations.comment: Subject-verb agreement error; change 'female' to 'females' for plural subject.

annotations.anchor_quote: By enlarging the femal mind is by virtue of educating them makes them better not only for their own good but also for the entire society.
annotations.correction: Enlarging the female mind through education makes them better not only for their own good but also for the entire society.
annotations.comment: Grammatically garbled sentence with typos; rephrase for clarity and correct 'femal' to 'female'.

annotations.anchor_quote: They question and rationalize and only than decide on whether to comply or not.
annotations.correction: They question and rationalize before deciding whether to comply or not.
annotations.comment: Repeats earlier idea of questioning and rationalizing; consolidate to eliminate redundancy.

annotations.anchor_quote: They suffer socially, politically and economically and generational cycles of women
annotations.correction: They suffer socially, politically, and economically, perpetuating generational cycles of deprivation.
annotations.comment: Paragraph ends abruptly and incompletely; complete the sentence and add transition to next section.

annotations.anchor_quote: deprivation neves break.
annotations.correction: deprivation never breaks.
annotations.comment: Correct misspelling 'neves' to 'never' to fix readability in opening sentence.

annotations.anchor_quote: However, hot all hope is lost
annotations.correction: However, not all hope is lost
annotations.comment: Change 'hot' to 'not' to correct obvious spelling error and improve clarity.

annotations.anchor_quote: what women cando, or can not;
annotations.correction: what women can do, or can not;
annotations.comment: Insert space in 'cando' to 'can do' for proper word separation and grammar.

annotations.anchor_quote: They do not let women think once for her own self and
annotations.correction: They do not let women think once for herself and
annotations.comment: Revise 'her own self' to 'herself' to fix pronoun agreement with plural 'women'.

annotations.anchor_quote: what women cando, or can not; what women can wear or wear not; what women can feel or feel not.
annotations.correction: Vary repetitive 'what women can/can not' structure.
annotations.comment: Reduce repetition in parallel phrases by varying wording to enhance conciseness.

annotations.anchor_quote: 3. In Pakistan, ensure implementation of Article 25, and Article 38
annotations.correction: Expand with analysis of how these articles address specific impediments like poverty or security.
annotations.comment: Sub-point is merely descriptive and lacks analytical depth; add cause-effect reasoning and examples to strengthen outline.

annotations.anchor_quote: Article 25, and Article 38
annotations.correction: Article 25A (right to education) and Article 38 (social justice principles)
annotations.comment: Reference to Article 25 is imprecise for education rights and lacks source attribution; specify Article 25A and cite constitution.

annotations.anchor_quote: NV. Conclusion.
annotations.correction: V. Conclusion.
annotations.comment: Typo in Roman numeral 'NV.' disrupts clarity; correct to 'V.' for proper outline structure.

annotations.anchor_quote: 3. In Pakistan, ensure implementation of Article 25, and Article 38
annotations.correction: 3. In Pakistan, enforce Article 25A and 38 to counter government provision gaps, backed by SDG alignment.
annotations.comment: Argument is weak and narrative; support with evidence like enforcement outcomes or comparisons to show critical analysis.

annotations.anchor_quote: women make 230% of world parliaments.
annotations.correction: women make up approximately 26% of world parliaments.
annotations.comment: Statistic exceeds 100% which is impossible; verify and correct with accurate UN data and specify the report source.

annotations.anchor_quote: plass amendment to protect women
annotations.correction: pass amendments to protect women
annotations.comment: Misspelling 'plass' should be 'pass' and make plural for grammatical accuracy; proofread for spelling errors.

annotations.anchor_quote: menace of povertythat hinders worven education.
annotations.correction: menace of poverty that hinders women's education.

annotations.anchor_quote: participate fully in the political process. ... that participate in the political process
annotations.correction: participate fully in the political process. ... that participate
annotations.comment: Repetition of 'participate in the political process'; remove redundant phrase to avoid repetition and improve conciseness.

annotations.anchor_quote: Despite the need and significance of female education, there are some mounting impediments that paralyze the process of women education.
annotations.correction: Despite the benefits outlined, significant impediments such as societal barriers paralyze female education.
annotations.comment: Abrupt shift from benefits to impediments lacks smooth transition; add linking phrase referencing prior points for better flow.

annotations.anchor_quote: Secondly, when female gets education, she secures economic sustenance for herself.
annotations.correction: Secondly, when a female gets education, she secures economic sustenance for herself.
annotations.comment: Add indefinite article 'a' before 'female' to correct subject-verb agreement and grammar.

annotations.anchor_quote: can make impartial, out of free will decision for the future.
annotations.correction: can make impartial decisions of her own free will for the future.
annotations.comment: Rephrase 'out of free will decision' to 'decisions of her own free will' for idiomatic correctness and plurality.

annotations.anchor_quote: provide for them According to UNFD
annotations.correction: provide for them. According to UNFPA
annotations.comment: Insert period after 'them' to separate sentences and capitalize next properly.

annotations.anchor_quote: According to UNFD (United Nations Population Fund) there is only 28% female labour force
annotations.correction: According to UNFPA (United Nations Population Fund), the female labor force participation rate is only 28%

annotations.anchor_quote: Thirdly, Females when edu- cated, are fully aware of their rights.
annotations.correction: Start a new paragraph for the third point on rights awareness.
annotations.comment: Split paragraph as it covers two ideas (economic independence and rights); begin new idea with topic sentence for better flow.

annotations.rubric_point: If the source of factual data is not mentioned, flag it.

annotations.correction: Protection of Women Against Workplace Harassment (2010), Acid Control and Acid Crime Prevention Act (2011), and Women Property Protection Rights Act (2020) [cite official source]

annotations.rubric_point: If the arguments in paragraphs are narrative or descriptive and don’t provide critical analysis through cause and effect relationship, flag them as weak arguments.
annotations.anchor_quote: She starts to think and live bey and them. She starts to believe that customs are only here to margin- alize cher and she, in result, does not give any importance or signifonce to them
annotations.correction: Educated females critically analyze customs as tools to marginalize them (cause), rejecting them to focus on personal goals (effect), as evidenced by [example].

annotations.anchor_quote: It is the women who nurture and polishes the next generation.

annotations.anchor_quote: resources to sponsers it.

annotations.anchor_quote: Firstly, there is need to understand that women educational empowerment is actually "human development".
annotations.correction: Firstly, there is a need to understand that women's educational empowerment is actually "human development".
annotations.comment: Missing article 'a' and possessive form; add 'a' before 'need' and 's after 'women' for correct grammar.

annotations.anchor_quote: There is a need to understand that women educational empowerment is actually "human development". There is a need to understand that women, if let like this, will only burdenize the world and the potential will go vain.
annotations.correction: There is a need to understand that women educational empowerment is actually "human development"; if neglected, women will only burden the world as their potential goes to waste.
annotations.comment: Repeated phrase 'There is a need to understand'; consolidate into one sentence to avoid repetition.

annotations.anchor_quote: This absolutely true in its essence.
annotations.correction: This is absolutely true in its essence.
annotations.comment: Missing verb 'is'; insert 'is' after 'This absolutely' to complete the sentence.

annotations.anchor_quote: Here, for Women there is need of "attitudinal Change", not more legislations void of enforcements.
annotations.correction: Here, for women there is a need for "attitudinal change", not more legislations void of enforcement.
annotations.comment: Incorrect capitalization, missing article 'a', and wrong plural; lowercase nouns, add 'a' before 'need', use 'change' and 'enforcement'.

annotations.anchor_quote: Here, for Women there is need of "attitudinal Change", not more legislations void of enforcements.
annotations.correction: Here, for women there is a need for attitudinal change, as it fosters societal acceptance of female education, unlike unenforced legislations that fail to address root causes.
annotations.comment: Descriptive claim lacks analysis; add cause-effect explanation on how attitudinal change overcomes impediments.

annotations.anchor_quote: Here, it is a big obst acl which needs to be eradicated at all costs for the safety of women and their progress.
